hypothesis: Introducing aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic residues at position 11 will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions with ECL2/TM5 while reducing off-target affinity to SSTR3/5.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs are failing primarily due to structural clashes and insufficient selectivity. Tightening structural constraints and focusing mutagenesis on non-pharmacophore positions should improve both structural quality and selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: Structural failures are likely due to clashes in the binding pocket. Lowering the clash threshold will enforce better geometry and reduce structural failures.

To improve selectivity, we need to disrupt off-target contacts at non-pharmacophore positions. Focusing design on these positions will allow us to introduce mutations that break interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 while preserving the native pharmacophore.
